title: hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. souliei
intro: Hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. souliei is a subspecies of the Hieracium genus, commonly known as hawkweed, a type of flowering plant native to various regions, including Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. souliei include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
